title: C Memory Outcomes, Evidence, and Toolchain Boundaries · C Contract Brief: Evidence Limits
summary: A civic sensor team must distinguish a passing browser model from evidence that its native C release is safe on supported targets. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem evidence limits increment in memory-safety evidence and transfer matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State what tests, warnings, analyzers, sanitizers, proofs, reviews, and production telemetry can and cannot establish.
Define spatial, temporal, initialization, type, ownership, resource, and concurrency properties as observable claims.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Evidence Limits · Theory Evidence Limits
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 8347. Produce bounded C evidence to separate browser evidence from native transfer evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: State what tests, warnings, analyzers, sanitizers, proofs, reviews, and production telemetry can and cannot establish.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Evidence Limits contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: Build Evidence Limits evidence · Theory Evidence Limits
steps.instruction:
For this theory demonstration of cmem-evidence-limits, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-evidence-limits-6m6z0c */" for changed case 6485. It must separate browser evidence from native transfer evidence, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails. Keep instant code within the PicoC 3.2.2 practice subset; do not claim C23, optimizing compiler, sanitizer, ABI, thread, operating-system, FFI, load, or production evidence until its explicit controlled transfer gate passes.

Evidence target: Build Evidence Limits evidence · Theory Evidence Limits.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Evidence Limits into observable performance evidence. For Build Evidence Limits evidence · Theory Evidence Limits, keep Evidence Limits connected to executable C memory invariant and explicit native transfer evidence.
